Title: Improving wood properties for wood utilization through multi-omics integration in lignin biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: PtrPAL family (PtrPAL1, PtrPAL2, PtrPAL3, PtrPAL4, PtrPAL5)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Abundantly expressed in stem-differentiating xylem fiber and vessel cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Abundantly expressed in stem-differentiating xylem fiber and vessel cells
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step from phenylalanine into monolignol pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid/monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Wood secondary cell walls in stem xylem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines wood properties important for timber, pulp, and biofuel processing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation reduces lignin and improves saccharification and carbohydrate:lignin ratio; PAL downregulation was predicted as the best single-family perturbation for overall wood quality improvement with limited growth penalty in selected scenarios
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Targeted suppression of PtrPAL genes generated broad reductions in transcript and protein abundance. 2. PAL downregulation strongly altered lignin content and multiple wood properties. 3. Integrative modeling predicted ~95% PAL downregulation as the best single-family strategy to improve saccharification and carbohydrate:lignin ratio, and PAL+CCoAOMT or PAL+C3H+CCoAOMT combinations for multi-trait improvement.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step supported by integrative analysis; strongest single-family engineering target
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; seasonal/environmental variation contributed to transcript abundance; saccharification evaluated with and without hot-water pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PAL family; aromatic amino acid lyase
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSE78953); proteomics by PC-IDMS; fluxomics/model-derived metabolic fluxes; 2D NMR lignin profiling; wood chemistry phenomics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: xylem-specific RNAi/amiRNA knockdown in transgenic Populus; RNA-seq; absolute protein quantification; kinetic modeling; lignin chemistry and saccharification phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for reducing lignin and improving saccharification and C:L ratio; can be combined with CCoAOMT and/or C3H perturbation for multi-trait engineering while monitoring growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Individual PAL family members are functionally redundant; family-level perturbation had larger effects than single-gene perturbation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong downregulation of monolignol genes can affect development, but some low-lignin PAL transgenics retained near-wildtype growth

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Abundantly expressed in stem-differentiating xylem fiber and vessel cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Abundantly expressed in stem-differentiating xylem fiber and vessel cells
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates trans-cinnamic acid at the 4-position to form p-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Wood secondary cell walls in stem xylem
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation reduces lignin content and changes lignin composition and wood traits; identified among influential perturbations for lignin, density, MOE, carbohydrate content and saccharification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4H family perturbation altered lignin content/composition and wood properties. 2. Integrative analysis ranked C4H among strong effectors for increasing or reducing specific wood traits depending on expression direction. 3. PtrC4H1/PtrC4H2 likely interact with PtrC3H3 as P450 complexes, but these interactions were not quantitatively incorporated into the model.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; seasonal/environmental transcript variation; saccharification with and without pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSE78953); proteomics by PC-IDMS; model-derived fluxomics; 2D NMR; wood chemistry phenomics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: xylem-specific knockdown; transcript and protein quantification; lignin/wood trait phenotyping; integrative modeling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for tuning lignin content, density, MOE, and saccharification, but requires caution due to possible growth effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein complex interactions with C3H were evidenced previously but omitted from kinetic equations because of assay limitations
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth/development effects can occur with monolignol gene downregulation

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxycinnamic acids including p-coumaric acid/caffeic acid/ferulic acid/5-hydroxyferulic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids to CoA thioesters using ATP
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Wood secondary cell walls in stem xylem
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation of 4CL family caused severe growth reduction and altered lignin and wood traits; 4CL perturbation ranked highly for modifying lignin, density, carbohydrate content and saccharification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL family perturbation strongly affected lignin biosynthesis and wood properties. 2. Ptr4CL3 and Ptr4CL5 form a heterotetrameric complex that alters CoA ligation specificity and rates and was incorporated into the kinetic model. 3. Downregulation of 4CL family can reduce lignin but often incurs severe growth reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step in modeled CoA ligation module
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; saccharification tested with and without pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSE78953); proteomics by PC-IDMS; kinetic/flux modeling; 2D NMR; wood phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic knockdown; transcript/protein quantification; incorporation of recombinant enzyme kinetics and 4CL complex behavior into mass-balance model; phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong lever for lignin engineering, but likely unsuitable alone when growth retention is required
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The Ptr4CL3 promoter was used as a xylem-specific promoter for transgene expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe growth reduction reported in Ptr4CL-family downregulated transgenics

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl moieties from CoA esters to shikimate/quinate intermediates in monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Wood secondary cell walls in stem xylem
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation altered lignin and wood traits and caused severe growth reduction in HCT-family transgenics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HCT family perturbation changed lignin content/composition and wood properties. 2. HCT-family downregulation caused severe growth reduction not strictly correlated with lignin content. 3. HCT ranked among strong effectors for lignin, S/G ratio, MOE, and carbohydrate-related traits.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; saccharification with and without pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSE78953); proteomics by PC-IDMS; model-derived fluxomics; 2D NMR; wood phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: xylem-specific knockdown; transcript/protein quantification; lignin and wood trait phenotyping; integrative modeling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can strongly shift lignin-related traits but growth penalty limits deployment unless combined strategically
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Recently discovered caffeoyl shikimate esterases were noted as future additions to the pathway model, relevant to this module

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates hydroxycinnamoyl esters at the 3-position to generate caffeoyl intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Wood secondary cell walls in stem xylem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls lignin amount/composition and downstream wood utilization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation reduces lignin and improves saccharification; combined PAL+C3H+CCoAOMT downregulation was predicted to give the best overall wood quality among tested scenarios
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtrC3H3 perturbation changed lignin composition and wood properties. 2. C3H family ranked highly for reducing lignin and increasing saccharification and carbohydrate content. 3. Combined downregulation with PAL and CCoAOMT gave the strongest predicted multi-trait improvement.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major control point suggested by engineering predictions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; saccharification with and without pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSE78953); proteomics by PC-IDMS; kinetic/flux modeling; 2D NMR; wood phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: xylem-specific knockdown; transcript/protein quantification; integrative systems modeling; lignin and saccharification phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target, especially in combinations with PAL and CCoAOMT for improving density, saccharification and C:L ratio
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: P450 complex interactions with C4H were known but not incorporated in flux equations
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible growth/development penalties depending on perturbation strength and combinations

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates caffeoyl-CoA to feruloyl-CoA using S-adenosylmethionine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Wood secondary cell walls in stem xylem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines lignin chemistry and biomass utilization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation, especially with PAL or with PAL+C3H, is predicted to markedly improve wood density, saccharification, and C:L ratio
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCoAOMT family perturbation altered lignin and wood traits. 2. PtrCCoAOMT3 showed poor transcript-protein predictive relationship compared with other pathway genes. 3. Combined downregulation of PAL and CCoAOMT, or PAL+C3H+CCoAOMT, gave top-ranked predicted multi-trait improvements.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: important control point suggested by combinatorial engineering predictions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; saccharification with and without pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSE78953); proteomics by PC-IDMS; kinetic/flux modeling; 2D NMR; wood phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: xylem-specific knockdown; RNA-seq; protein quantification; integrative modeling; lignin and saccharification analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent combinatorial engineering target with PAL and C3H for pulp/biofuel-oriented wood quality improvement
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transcript abundance of PtrCCoAOMT3 was a poor predictor of protein abundance (R2 ~0.03), implying additional regulation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential off-target developmental impacts if strongly suppressed

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Reduction of hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA esters to aldehydes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA esters to the corresponding aldehydes using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Wood secondary cell walls in stem xylem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects lignin amount/composition, stiffness, and saccharification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CCR-family downregulation reduced lignin, caused severe growth reduction, and strongly affected wood density, β-O-4 linkages, and saccharification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCR family perturbation strongly changed lignin amount/composition and wood traits. 2. PtrCCR-family downregulation caused severe growth reduction. 3. Increased hydroxycinnamaldehyde incorporation into lignin in a transgenic line was associated with large MOE reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; saccharification with and without pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSE78953); proteomics by PC-IDMS; model-derived fluxomics; 2D NMR; wood phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: xylem-specific knockdown; transcript/protein quantification; lignin chemistry; MOE and saccharification phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for reducing recalcitrance, but severe growth penalty limits practical deployment unless compensated
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Hydroxycinnamaldehyde accumulation in lignin can markedly weaken wood stiffness
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe growth reduction; weakened mechanical properties

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydroxycinnamaldehydes to the corresponding monolignol alcohols using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Wood secondary cell walls in stem xylem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines lignin monomer composition and linkage properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CAD perturbation alters lignin composition, S/G ratio, β-O-4 linkages, and mechanical properties; hydroxycinnamaldehyde incorporation can reduce MOE
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CAD perturbation changed lignin chemistry and wood traits. 2. The study used a construct simultaneously overexpressing PtrCAD2 and downregulating PtrCAD1, indicating functional distinction between family members. 3. Elevated aldehyde-derived units in lignin were associated with major reduction in mechanical strength.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant of monolignol alcohol formation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSE78953); proteomics by PC-IDMS; 2D NMR; wood phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: xylem-specific knockdown/overexpression construct; transcript/protein quantification; lignin NMR and wood mechanical analyses
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can tune lignin composition, but must avoid excessive aldehyde accumulation that weakens wood
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CAD influences the proportion of hydroxycinnamaldehyde-derived residues in lignin
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced mechanical strength when aldehyde-rich lignin accumulates

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates guaiacyl-pathway intermediates at the 5-position to direct flux toward syringyl lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Wood secondary cell walls in stem xylem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls S/G ratio and associated wood properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CAld5H perturbation strongly altered S/G ratio and was ranked among top perturbations for density, MOE, β-O-4 linkage, and lignin traits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CAld5H1/2 perturbation substantially changed lignin S/G ratio and composition. 2. Integrative analysis identified CAld5H as an important effector for multiple desired wood traits. 3. Family-level perturbation had stronger effects than individual genes, indicating redundancy.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSE78953); proteomics by PC-IDMS; kinetic/flux modeling; 2D NMR; wood phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: xylem-specific knockdown; transcript/protein quantification; lignin NMR composition analysis; integrative modeling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for shifting S/G ratio and improving specific wood-processing traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named CAld5H in this study, equivalent to F5H activity in monolignol pathway
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential indirect effects on growth/adaptation if strongly perturbed

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates 5-hydroxylated lignin intermediates to promote syringyl monolignol formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Wood secondary cell walls in stem xylem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Influences lignin composition and wood utilization traits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AldOMT perturbation influenced lignin content, MOE, density, and saccharification; ranked highly for several desired wood traits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AldOMT family perturbation altered lignin and wood properties. 2. PtrAldOMT2 transgenes indirectly affected PtrPAL1 expression, illustrating pathway gene interactions. 3. Integrative analysis ranked AldOMT among key perturbation points for multiple desired traits.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSE78953); proteomics by PC-IDMS; kinetic/flux modeling; wood phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: xylem-specific knockdown; transcript/protein quantification; integrative modeling; lignin and wood trait analyses
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning mechanical strength and saccharification, alone or in combinations
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper uses AldOMT/AldoMT naming interchangeably in trait-ranking figure text
